rulings.Case Title: PORFERIO EBORLAS VS. INDYMAC BANK, FSB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 17, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court has determined that Defendants do not need to bring this as a Motion to Reconsider and rules as follows on the Demurrer. Defendants¿ Demurrer on the 5th cause of action (Breach of Covenant of Good Faith & Fair Dealing) is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts showing a contractual relationship and the elements of this cause of action. Although Defendants were not a party when the contract was originally created, they are still bound by the covenant of good faith and fair dealing since they are now trying to enforce the deed of trust and promissory note. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Civil Conspiracy) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to plead the formation and operation of the conspiracy as to these defendants and the specific wrongful acts done pursuant to the alleged conspiracy. Because this was the second opportunity provided to have Plaintiffs do so and they have not, the Court does not think that further leave will change that result. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (BP §17200 Violation) is overruled. On 4.13.11 the Court overruled the Demurrer as to this cause of action and ordered that Plaintiff distinguish the conduct of each Defendant which Plaintiff has now done. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 8th (Unjust Enrichment), 9th (Quiet Title), 10th (Rescission) and 11th (Injunctive Relief) causes of action is overruled. The Court previously overruled the Demurrers as to this cause of action and Defendant¿s arguments under FIRREA and the D¿Oench Duhme Doctrine are not applicable as to these Defendants only IndyMac. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NAVISTAR, INC. VS. CALIFORNIA AIR RESOURCES BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 17, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT MARY NICHOLS, CALIFORNIA AIR RESOURCES BOARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Cummins Inc et al¿s Demurrer to the 1st coa is overruled. The arguments raised require the Court to interpret the Settlement Agreement and its requirement. The Court usually refrains from doing so one demurrer because the interpretation of any contract is a factual one not appropriate at the Demurrer stage. All Defendants¿ Demurrers to the 2nd coa are overruled. As to the standing argument, under the Waste Management of Alameda County and Save the Plastic Bag Coalition cases Plaintiff does have standing. Plaintiff does more than assert an economic or competitive interest. Plaintiff alleges at various places within the F.A.C. that Defendant CARB¿s interpretation and application of the rules allows for SCR engines to pollute the air for extended periods of time. This is sufficient to establish citizen standing. As to the Res Judicata argument, while some of the allegations in this complaint and the prior action are similar, the declarations sought are not and the instant complaint relates to actions that occurred after the previous suit was settled. Furthermore, it appears that paragraph 6 of the Settlement Agreement was formed to permit this type of action. Finally, Defendant CARB argues that this action should have been brought by Writ of Mandate. As a general rule administrative decisions are reviewed by a petition for Writ. However, there is an exception to this rule when the declaratory relief cause of action is to challenge the agency¿s policy of ignoring or violating applicable laws and regulation. East Bay Muni. Util. Dist. V. Dept. of Forestry & Fire Protection (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 1113,1119. Because Plaintiff alleges in the complaint that Defendant CARB is not correctly interpreting and enforcing certain statutory requirements under Ca. law, they meet the exception to the rule. =(302/LMG)
